Yeah, the real estate market is a great place to go right now – not helping people get into
real estate, but helping people who are in it move properties. Help people sell houses
and find prospects and that kind of thing. If you put your mind to it and think ‘recession,’
think about the current economic environment and how things might go from here, you will
come up with others.
I think retirees are going to be more and more pinched as the cost of living continues to
rise. They’re on fixed incomes. Every time the cost of living rises, it’s not just that things
cost more. It means they can’t buy as much. They can’t live as well. Anything that you can
do to help those people would be great.
4. 	Is there any special or unique way to connect with these potential
entrepreneurs?
The obvious way, really, is online search. People are going to come to the web. They are
going to type in ‘home-based businesses and they’re going to type in ‘self employment’
and ‘entrepreneurship’ and those kinds of search phrases.
If you have a pay per click ad at the top of the search page or if you have a highly rated
sales page that’s been optimized for the search engines and you appear in the first page
or two, you’re going to get traffic from it.
Another way would be to go to sites that cater to entrepreneurs and opportunity seekers.
Look at ezine sponsorships where you do a deal. You say, “Here’s some money. You’re
going to put an ad in your ezine for my website that shows people how to do XYZ. In
addition, you’re going to blast the file once or twice.”
ArcaMax and NewsMax are great sites for that kind of thing. They put these amazing
deals together. For $2,000-$3,000 on ArcaMax you can have an email that you write
about your product or service blasted to 1.6 million people. There are a bunch of residual
benefits that go along with it as well.

-- 32 of 36 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 6 • Makepeace Secrets Exposed! Q&A Session 1
Transcript 	Page 33 of 36
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
Also I would like to suggest that you send an email to Julie McManus at our blog because
her specialty is driving traffic through paid campaigns. She’ll have a whole bunch of other
ideas for you.
I hope that helps.
The next question is from Phillip:
Regarding customer acquisition, is there a place for giving away free front-
ends to acquire a new name?
Absolutely. That’s what we do all the time on the web. There are all kinds of permutations
of this, Phillip. One is a 60-day free trial of a product, which is essentially a soft offer. Then
you bill them after 60 days. Another way to do it is to ask people to send for your free
magalog – which is an advertorial that delivers value but also sells a product.
Online is where freebies rule. Looking around the web you’ll see it all the time.
The second part of your question is about loss leaders. Yes, we are very happy to spend
a bunch of money on pay per click or paid campaigns so long as at the end of 60 or 90 or
even 120 days we have our money back that we spent because those people have bought
stuff from us. We also have thousands of new names. It’s very valid, absolutely.
Test your way into it because the key is how long it’s going to take to get your money back.
You’re going to find that some sources you’re never going to get your money back from
because the people they send you are going to be absolute deadbeats. You’re going to
find that other sources are really great. You need to be cautious and test your way into it
just because of this lag.
Here are some times to consider multi-step offline campaigns. This would be to produce
the lead by offering something free. Then follow up with a sales piece or a call or
something. These are times that you might want to consider that:
• 	When your product price is huge
• 	When you need to call prospects from a large universe cheaply before presenting
your full sales message. Let’s say your full sales message comes in a $2 per piece
presentation package. Maybe you’re selling commercial loans or something like
that. You don’t want to send that to the phone book. You don’t want to send it to a
bunch of people who may or may not be in the market for a loan right now.
You can send a postcard to a huge file very cheaply or as part of an insert program
and go out to lots and lots of prospects. What happens is only the people that are

-- 33 of 36 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 6 • Makepeace Secrets Exposed! Q&A Session 1
Transcript 	Page 3 of 36
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
interested at some level raise their hand and say, “This is something I’m interested
in. I’m qualified. I think I can do this. Why don’t you tell me more?”
Then you can go ahead and send your DVD or your direct mail piece or make a
phone call to the person. Do whatever’s necessary to move them along the buying
process.
• 	Sometimes a live sales person is required to answer questions and address
objections.
• 	Sometimes closing a sale will require more than a simple order form. You may need
to take them through a big, long application of some kind. That may require live
help.
• 	In some cases the product is an infinitely customized one. I’m not talking about
colors and size here like with clothes. I’m talking about if you’re selling plant
equipment or office equipment or some other thing that requires you to ascertain
information about the prospect prior to advising them on which version of the
product or which options to buy.
Those are just some of the reasons why people use two-step programs. Two-step
programs – just in terms of cost outlay – are, by their nature, more expensive than one-
step programs. Each step costs you money to send and, of course, the first step doesn’t
result in a sale.
A good example is there was this fantastic Smith & Hemmings promotion I saw when I
lived in L.A. They were trying to sell commercial loans. You’re a business person and you
got this little, tiny envelope. It said, “Free golf ball enclosed.” Of course it’s a flat envelope.
Everyone says, “Ha, ha!” and they opened it.
The next week you got a little bit bigger and it said, “Free tennis ball enclosed”. Every
week for the next eight or nine weeks the balls got bigger. Finally at the end of the process
you got this big thing with a life-sized picture of a basketball on it.
In each communication there was a message driving people to the loan officers. They
could afford to do that. They had to do something like that because competition was so
high for commercial loans. They needed visibility. They needed frequency in the mailbox.
They could afford to do it because the amount of money a bank makes on a multi-million
dollar business loan is more than enough to offset the cost of that kind of a campaign.
Those are some of the considerations to keep in mind. Times to consider multi-step
campaigns online: always. They’re free.

-- 34 of 36 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 6 • Makepeace Secrets Exposed! Q&A Session 1
Transcript 	Page 3 of 36
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
Absence does not make the heart grow fonder online. The more that you can contact
customers and deliver content of value in those contacts, the tighter the relationship you’re
going to have with that customer.
The next question is from Andrew:
When it comes to premium offer summary pages – the kind of thing we put in
acquisition promo right before the response device – what are your thoughts
on the pro’s and con’s of showing your best offer versus all three options?
Just so you know what Andrew’s talking about, this is an inside back cover where it faces
the order form on a health resources promotion. You’ll notice on here that it shows all of
this great free stuff you get. It’s $267 in free gifts and discounts.
Nowhere on this page does it mention that you have to go with the largest order – which
was a six-month supply or something – in order to receive all of this. It was a graduated
offer. Progressively as you ordered the next two echelons down you got fewer freebies
and discounts.
In this one, you get extra bottles of the main product for ordering. You get this extra
supplement, which are minerals that compliment the main product. You get four bottles if
you select the highest amount. You get these three free reports.
My philosophy is to put your best foot forward. Explain the details on the order form. Also
explain the details in the running copy – the offer copy and the promotion piece. On this
page where you’re trying to put your best foot forward, you want to show everything. You
want to overwhelm them with the value of the offer. You want to push them towards your
best offer.
Jonathan said:
Speaking of the real estate market, as an investor in commercial properties
myself, I’ve noticed many vacancies with fellow owners’ properties in the
last three to six months. All these vacancies stem from the real estate
contraction. Realtors, real estate loan companies, escrow companies, and
mortgage brokers…
Yeah. All of the above are great targets right now in this market. Basically it’s about
desperation, isn’t it? It’s about the intensity of the fear or the intensity of the desire. It’s
either the fear of succumbing to a particular problem or the desire to overcome it.
Develop a nose for fear. Look for the people to whom your service will have the greatest
value, to whom your marketing ideas and your copy are in most dire need. Those are the

-- 35 of 36 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 6 • Makepeace Secrets Exposed! Q&A Session 1
Transcript 	Page 36 of 36
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
people who would be the most willing to partner with you and offer you great incentives
– including a piece of the action – to work with.
Jonathan also says:
Always test…
He’s talking about the loss leader offer.
Offering free reports, start with the free report and then continue after the
email is submitted. Also offer benefit for the content.
Right. Absolutely.
All right, I think we’ve pretty much covered it all. Wow, you guys are great. Have a great
rest of the day. Goodbye.
This ends Module 6

-- 36 of 36 --
